scenarios.name: PRD breakdown for e-commerce platform
scenarios.input:
Analyze this PRD and create an implementation plan:

# PRD: Customer Loyalty Program

We want to add a loyalty points system to our e-commerce platform. Customers earn points on purchases, can redeem points for discounts, and see their tier status (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Admins need to configure point-earning rules and manage tier thresholds. We use React frontend, Node.js/Express backend, PostgreSQL. The mobile app team will need an API. Target launch is 8 weeks.

Please break this down into epics, stories, and tasks with estimates.

eval_criteria.criterion: Creates epic-level groupings (not just a flat task list)
Stories have acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format
Tasks are sized at 1-8 hours each (no task exceeds 8h)
Assigns priority levels using P0/P1/P2/P3 classification
Identifies infrastructure-first tasks (DB schema, auth setup) that must precede feature tasks
Maps dependencies between tasks (blocked-by/blocks relationships)
Identifies a critical path with minimum duration estimate

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT produce a single flat list of tasks without hierarchy
Does NOT leave all tasks at the same priority level
Does NOT create tasks larger than 8 hours without splitting them

scenarios.name: PRD breakdown for SaaS MVP
scenarios.input:
Break down this concept into an implementation plan with pokayokay's planning methodology:

# Product Brief: Team Standup Bot

Slack bot that collects async standups from team members. Each morning it DMs team members asking: what they did yesterday, what they're doing today, blockers. Summarizes responses in a channel. Tracks participation over time. Dashboard for managers.

Stack: TypeScript, Next.js, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Slack Bolt SDK.

Create the full epic/story/task hierarchy.

eval_criteria.criterion: Creates 2+ epics grouping related capabilities
Each story follows INVEST criteria (independent, valuable, testable)
Tasks include ohno-compatible descriptions (behavior, input/output contract, acceptance criteria, connections)
Assigns P0-P3 priorities with rationale
Identifies skill routing for tasks (e.g., api-design, database-design, testing-strategy)
Estimates each task in hours

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT skip acceptance criteria on stories
Task descriptions are self-contained (not just titles)
Does NOT assign all tasks the same priority

scenarios.name: Classify features by P0-P3 priority
scenarios.input:
I have these features for a project management tool. Classify each as P0, P1, P2, or P3 with rationale:

1. User login/registration
2. Create and assign tasks
3. Dark mode toggle
4. Email notifications for task updates
5. Gantt chart visualization
6. Role-based access control (admin, member, viewer)
7. CSV export of tasks
8. Real-time collaborative editing
9. Task comments and @mentions
10. Mobile responsive layout

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses exactly the P0/P1/P2/P3 classification scheme (not High/Medium/Low or MoSCoW alone)
P0 items are true MVP launch blockers (product doesn't work without them)
P3 items are explicitly deferred/out-of-scope for initial build
Each classification includes a rationale explaining why
Distinguishes between P1 (significantly impacts value) and P2 (nice-to-have)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT classify everything as High priority
Does NOT use a different priority scheme than P0-P3

scenarios.name: Flag ambiguities in a vague concept brief
scenarios.input:
Analyze this concept brief and flag all ambiguities, assumptions, and open questions before planning:

# Concept: AI Writing Assistant

We want to build an AI-powered writing tool. Users write content and the AI helps improve it. Should support multiple content types. Needs to be fast and reliable. We want it to feel premium.

Identify what's missing and what questions need answers before we can plan implementation.

eval_criteria.criterion: Identifies missing success metrics / measurable outcomes
Flags undefined user personas (who are the target users?)
Questions what 'multiple content types' means specifically
Questions what 'fast' means in measurable terms (latency targets)
Identifies missing technical constraints (stack, AI model, hosting)
Documents assumptions explicitly (not just questions)
Separates ambiguities into categories (scope, technical, business)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT jump straight to implementation plan without flagging gaps
Does NOT treat vague language as clear requirements

scenarios.name: Route features to appropriate skills
scenarios.input:
I'm planning implementation of these features. For each one, recommend which pokayokay skill should handle it and why:

1. REST API for user management (CRUD + auth)
2. PostgreSQL schema for multi-tenant data
3. CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions
4. Stripe payment integration
5. React dashboard with charts
6. AWS Lambda for webhook processing
7. End-to-end test suite with Playwright
8. OpenAPI documentation generation

eval_criteria.criterion: Routes REST API work to api-design skill
Routes database schema work to database-design skill
Routes CI/CD work to ci-cd skill
Routes AWS Lambda/cloud work to cloud-infrastructure skill
Routes testing work to testing-strategy skill
Identifies secondary skills where applicable (e.g., testing-strategy as secondary for API)
Mentions skill names matching pokayokay's actual skill inventory

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT invent skill names that don't exist in pokayokay
Does NOT route all features to a single generic skill

scenarios.name: Write self-contained task descriptions for ohno
scenarios.input:
Write a task description for an ohno task that an implementer agent will receive as its only context. The task is: 'Create the user registration API endpoint' for a Node.js/Express app with PostgreSQL.

The description must be completely self-contained since the implementer gets no other context.

eval_criteria.criterion: Includes explicit behavior section describing what the code does
Includes input/output contract (request body shape, response shape, status codes)
Includes acceptance criteria as checkboxes
Includes 'Connects To' section with dependency context
Includes 'Patterns to Follow' pointing to existing code conventions
Description is long enough to be self-contained (100+ words)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT produce a one-liner task description
Does NOT omit status codes from the endpoint contract
Description mentions specific file paths or code patterns to follow

scenarios.name: Split an oversized story into smaller stories
scenarios.input:
This story is too large. Split it into smaller stories using appropriate splitting patterns:

'As an admin, I want to manage all aspects of the product catalog so that products are organized and discoverable. This includes creating products with images, setting prices and inventory, managing categories and tags, configuring search indexing, setting up product recommendations, and managing product reviews.'

eval_criteria.criterion: Splits into 3+ separate stories
Each split story has its own acceptance criteria
Uses a recognized splitting pattern (workflow steps, simple/complex, data variations)
Each split story is independently deliverable (provides value on its own)
Identifies the out-of-scope items for each split story

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT keep it as one large story
Split stories are not just the original bullet points restated as story titles

scenarios.name: Identify task dependencies and critical path
scenarios.input:
Given these tasks for a blog platform feature, identify all dependencies (blocked-by relationships) and determine the critical path:

1. Create blog_posts database table (2h)
2. Create Post model with validation (3h)
3. Build POST /api/posts endpoint (4h)
4. Build GET /api/posts endpoint with pagination (3h)
5. Create PostList React component (4h)
6. Create PostEditor React component (6h)
7. Wire PostEditor to POST API (2h)
8. Wire PostList to GET API (2h)
9. Add image upload to posts (4h)
10. Write integration tests (3h)

eval_criteria.criterion: Correctly identifies DB table as blocking the model task
Correctly identifies model as blocking API endpoints
Correctly identifies API endpoints as blocking frontend wiring tasks
Presents dependencies as a graph or matrix (not just prose)
Identifies the critical path with total duration
Identifies which tasks can run in parallel

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT treat all tasks as sequential (some can be parallelized)
Critical path duration is calculated correctly based on dependency chain

scenarios.name: Generate PROJECT.md output format
scenarios.input:
I've analyzed a PRD for a notification service. Generate the PROJECT.md file that should go in .claude/PROJECT.md. Include:
- 2 epics (Core Notifications, Advanced Features)
- 3 stories per epic
- 2-3 tasks per story

Use pokayokay's standard output format.

eval_criteria.criterion: Output targets .claude/PROJECT.md path
Includes epic definitions with ID, priority, goal, scope, and success criteria
Stories use 'As a / I want / So that' format
Tasks include type (frontend/backend/etc), hour estimates, and blocked-by fields
Includes a dependency graph section
Includes a timeline/phasing section

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT place output files outside .claude/ directory
Epic and story IDs follow a consistent naming scheme

scenarios.name: Detect infrastructure-first tasks in a PRD
scenarios.input:
This PRD describes a new project from scratch. Identify which infrastructure tasks must be created first and what they block:

# PRD: Inventory Management System

Build a web app for warehouse inventory tracking. Features: product CRUD, barcode scanning, stock level alerts, role-based access (warehouse staff, managers, admins), reporting dashboard. Stack: React, NestJS, PostgreSQL, Redis for caching.

No existing codebase - this is greenfield.

eval_criteria.criterion: Identifies database schema setup as a blocking infrastructure task
Identifies auth/RBAC setup as a blocking infrastructure task
Identifies test framework setup as a blocking infrastructure task
Shows which feature tasks are blocked by each infrastructure task
Orders infrastructure tasks before feature tasks in the plan

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT interleave infrastructure and feature tasks without dependency ordering
Does NOT skip test setup for a greenfield project

scenarios.name: Estimate tasks with complexity adjustments
scenarios.input:
Estimate these tasks in hours. For each, state your base estimate and any adjustment factors:

1. Add email field validation to existing user form (React + Zod)
2. Integrate Stripe subscription billing (new integration, never done before)
3. Migrate legacy MySQL queries to Prisma ORM
4. Build admin dashboard with 5 chart widgets
5. Add i18n support to 30 existing components

eval_criteria.criterion: Provides hour-based estimates for each task
Applies adjustment factors for new technology (+50-100%)
Applies adjustment factors for legacy integration (+30-50%)
Flags any task that exceeds 8 hours as needing to be split
Gives confidence ranges (not single-point estimates)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT give all tasks the same estimate
Does NOT ignore the 'never done before' factor for Stripe integration

scenarios.name: Gap analysis on an incomplete PRD
scenarios.input:
Perform a gap analysis on this PRD. What's missing, what assumptions would you make, and what questions must be answered before planning?

# PRD: Employee Directory

Build an internal employee directory. Should show org chart, allow searching by name/department/skills, and let employees update their profiles. Needs to sync with our HR system.

eval_criteria.criterion: Identifies missing success metrics
Questions which HR system and what sync mechanism (API, CSV, real-time)
Flags missing auth/access control requirements
Documents explicit assumptions (not just questions)
Separates findings into categories (missing info, assumptions, questions)
Includes a completeness checklist or quality assessment

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT treat the brief as complete and jump to implementation
Questions are specific (not generic 'what are the requirements')

scenarios.name: Break down a simple feature (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: Break down this feature into implementable tasks with estimates: 'Add a dark mode toggle to the settings page of our React app. We already have a ThemeProvider and light theme defined.'

eval_criteria.criterion: Creates 2+ separate tasks
Each task has an hour estimate
Tasks are ordered by dependency
Includes a testing task

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT create a single monolithic task
Estimates are reasonable (total under 16h for a toggle feature)

scenarios.name: Write a user story with acceptance criteria (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: Write a user story with acceptance criteria for: password reset functionality via email.

eval_criteria.criterion: Uses 'As a / I want / So that' format
Includes 3+ acceptance criteria
Acceptance criteria are testable (not vague)
Mentions edge cases (expired link, user not found)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT write acceptance criteria as implementation steps

scenarios.name: Consolidate scattered requirements into unified plan
scenarios.input:
These requirements came from different stakeholders in separate messages. Consolidate them into a unified analysis, flag contradictions, and propose a plan:

From PM: 'Users need to upload documents (PDF, Word). Max 10MB. Need full-text search.'
From CTO: 'Use S3 for storage. Keep costs low - maybe use cheaper storage tier.'
From Designer: 'Drag and drop upload with progress bar. Preview PDFs inline.'
From Security: 'All documents must be encrypted at rest. Scan for malware before storing.'
From PM (later): 'Actually, let's support files up to 50MB for enterprise users.'

eval_criteria.criterion: Identifies the contradiction in file size limits (10MB vs 50MB)
Consolidates all sources into a single requirements view
Flags cost vs feature tension (cheap storage vs malware scanning + encryption)
Assigns priorities to the consolidated features
Lists open questions that need resolution before planning

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT ignore the contradicting file size requirements
Does NOT treat all stakeholder inputs as equally prioritized
